description: Add @tanstack/react-query as a dependency to all 7 affected apps and set up QueryClientProvider wrappers so pages can use React Query hooks. Create a shared query-provider component in packages/shared-ui or as a layout wrapper in each app.
steps: Add @tanstack/react-query ^5.60.0 and @tanstack/react-query-devtools ^5 to apps/quantai/package.json, apps/quantchat/package.json, apps/quantmail/package.json, apps/quantedits/package.json, apps/quantmax/package.json, apps/quantneon/package.json, apps/quantube/package.json

Create a QueryProvider component (e.g. src/providers/query-provider.tsx) in each app that wraps children with QueryClientProvider and configures default options (staleTime: 30000, retry: 3, refetchOnWindowFocus: false)

Wrap each app's root layout with the QueryProvider: for App Router apps (quantai, quantchat, quantmail) wrap in src/app/layout.tsx; for pages-dir apps (quantedits, quantmax, quantneon, quantube) wrap in _app.tsx or create one if missing
Run pnpm install to resolve dependencies
Run pnpm typecheck to verify no type errors introduced
acceptance_criteria: @tanstack/react-query is listed in dependencies of all 7 apps
Each app has a QueryClientProvider wrapping its component tree
pnpm typecheck passes for all affected apps
findings: Pages-dir apps (quantedits, quantmax, quantneon, quantube) do not have Next.js as a dependency, so _app.tsx uses a local AppProps interface instead of importing from next/app. Pre-existing type errors exist in these apps (unused vars, possibly-undefined) but pnpm typecheck passes because they lack a typecheck script in their package.json. The turbo pipeline (82 tasks) passes cleanly.
